description: FutSpring Authentication — register, login, JWT handling, protected routes

userStories.title: Backend project scaffold: Spring Boot + PostgreSQL
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a working Spring Boot project with PostgreSQL connection so that subsequent stories can build on it.
acceptanceCriteria: Maven project created under `backend/` with Spring Boot 3.x, Spring Data JPA, Spring Security, Spring Web, PostgreSQL driver, Lombok, Spring Validation
`application.properties` (or `application.yml`) configures datasource from env vars: DB_URL, DB_USERNAME, DB_PASSWORD
JWT secret configurable via env var JWT_SECRET (default value provided for dev)
JWT expiry configurable via env var JWT_EXPIRATION_MS (default 604800000 = 7 days)
App starts without errors when DB is available
Global exception handler returns `{ status, message, timestamp }` shape
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend User entity and database schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the User entity and database table so that auth can store and retrieve user accounts.
acceptanceCriteria: User JPA entity with fields: id (Long, auto), email (unique, not null), username (not null), password (not null, hashed), image (String, nullable), backgroundImage (String, nullable), stars (int, default 0), position (String, nullable)
UserRepository extends JpaRepository with findByEmail method
Flyway or Hibernate DDL auto creates the users table on startup
No direct entity serialization — UserResponseDTO created with: id, email, username, image, backgroundImage, stars, position
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend auth endpoints: register and login
userStories.description: As a developer, I need POST /api/v1/auth/register and POST /api/v1/auth/login endpoints so that users can create accounts and obtain JWTs.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/auth/register accepts { username, email, password }, validates with @Valid, hashes password with BCrypt, saves user, returns { token, user: UserResponseDTO }
POST /api/v1/auth/login accepts { email, password }, validates credentials, returns { token, user: UserResponseDTO }
JWT contains userId and email as claims, signed with JWT_SECRET, expires after JWT_EXPIRATION_MS
Returns 400 with field errors if validation fails
Returns 409 if email already registered
Returns 401 if login credentials are wrong
/api/v1/auth/** is publicly accessible (no auth required)
Rate limiting on POST /api/v1/auth/login (10 requests per minute per IP)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend Spring Security JWT filter
userStories.description: As the system, I need a JWT filter so that all protected endpoints validate bearer tokens and reject unauthenticated requests.
acceptanceCriteria: OncePerRequestFilter reads Authorization header, validates JWT, populates SecurityContext
All routes under /api/** (except /api/v1/auth/**) require a valid JWT
Missing or invalid token returns 401 with { status, message, timestamp }
SecurityConfig disables CSRF (stateless API), sets session to STATELESS
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend project scaffold: Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a working frontend project so that subsequent UI stories can build on it.
acceptanceCriteria: Vite + React 18 + TypeScript project created under `frontend/`
Tailwind CSS configured
shadcn/ui initialized with at least Button, Input, Label, Tabs, Card components added
Sonner (toast) configured and available app-wide
React Router v6 configured with lazy-loaded routes and Suspense fallback
Axios installed; base API client configured with baseURL from VITE_API_URL env var (default http://localhost:8080)
App renders without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend AuthContext, useAuth hook, and Axios interceptors
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a global auth state and Axios interceptors so that the token is attached to every request and 401s redirect to /auth.
acceptanceCriteria: AuthContext provides: user (UserResponseDTO | null), token (string | null), login(token, user), logout()
useAuth hook exposes the context
On app load, token and user are read from localStorage to restore session
Axios request interceptor attaches Authorization: Bearer <token> header if token exists
Axios response interceptor calls logout() and redirects to /auth on 401
Auth API functions created: registerUser(data), loginUser(data) calling the backend endpoints
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend auth page: register and login forms with tabs
userStories.description: As a user, I want to register or log in from a single page with two tabs so that I can access FutSpring.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /auth renders an auth page with two tabs: Login and Register
Active tab reflected in URL query param: /auth?tab=login or /auth?tab=register
Register tab has fields: username, email, password, confirmPassword — all required
Register validates: password >= 8 chars, confirmPassword matches password; shows inline errors
Login tab has fields: email, password — all required
On successful register: stores token+user, redirects to /home
On successful login: stores token+user, redirects to /home
API errors shown as toast (e.g. email taken, wrong credentials)
Loading state on submit buttons during API call
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend protected routes and /home placeholder
userStories.description: As the system, I want unauthenticated users redirected to /auth and authenticated users kept on protected pages.
acceptanceCriteria: PrivateRoute component redirects to /auth if no token in AuthContext
After login, user is redirected back to originally requested route (or /home by default)
Routes /home, /pelada/:id, /daily/:id, /profile/:id are wrapped with PrivateRoute
/home renders a placeholder page (e.g. 'My Peladas - coming soon') so routing works
/ (landing) renders a simple public placeholder page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend logout
userStories.description: As a user, I want to log out so that my session is cleared and I am redirected to /auth.
acceptanceCriteria: Logout button visible on authenticated pages (e.g. in a top nav/header)
Clicking logout calls logout() from useAuth, clears localStorage, redirects to /auth
After logout, navigating to a protected route redirects to /auth
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
